analysis.reasoning: Core message: EU leaders are coordinating on COVID-19, stressing international solidarity for vaccine access and discussing COVAX with WHO, with the EU pledging to contribute. Evaluation: EU1 (successful story) – not directly; no mention of history or overcoming internal EU obstacles. EU2 (EU is united) – implied coordination (EU leaders VTC), but unity is not explicitly highlighted; still weakly relevant but primary focus is external/global action rather than intra-EU unity. EU3 (useful for its citizens within EU) – focus is global vaccine access, not specifically EU citizens. EU4 (avant-garde) – not about pioneering regulation or leading edge policies per se. EU5 (global champion of fair causes) – promoting fair global vaccine access could touch human-rights-equity framing, but the tweet frames it as solidarity to overcome the pandemic rather than rights advocacy; marginal. EU6 (EU contributes to a better world) – clearly aligns: emphasis on international solidarity, multilateral cooperation (COVAX with WHO), and the EU committing to play its part globally. Final determination: EU6.
